# Command Reference

Comprehensive reference of every command available in this project.
Auto-generated from Taskfile definitions, script `--help` output, and
Hatch environment configuration.

!!! tip "Quick start"
    Most developers only need `task check` (run all quality gates) and
    `task test` (run tests). See [Developer Commands](../development/developer-commands.md)
    for a guided walkthrough.

## Taskfile Commands

Run any task with `task <name>`. Pass extra arguments with `-- <args>` (e.g., `task test -- -v`).

### General

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task bootstrap` | One-command setup for fresh clones |
| `task build` | Build source and wheel distributions |
| `task check` | Run all quality checks (lint, format, typecheck, test) |
| `task commit` | Interactive conventional commit (commitizen) |
| `task customize` | Interactive project customization |
| `task default` | List available tasks |
| `task deptry` | Check for unused, missing, or transitive dependencies |
| `task doctor` | Print diagnostics bundle for bug reports |
| `task fmt` | Apply formatting |
| `task info` | Show project metadata |
| `task lint` | Check for linting issues |
| `task run` | Run the application |
| `task security` | Run bandit security linter |
| `task shell` | Enter the Hatch dev shell |
| `task test` | Run tests |
| `task typecheck` | Run mypy type checker |
| `task version` | Show project version |

### Actions

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task actions:check` | CI gate: exit non-zero if any actions are stale or upgradable |
| `task actions:update-comments` | Update version comments on SHA-pinned actions in workflow files |
| `task actions:upgrade` | Upgrade SHA-pinned actions (usage: task actions:upgrade, or: task actions:upgrade -- actions/checkout v6.1.0) |
| `task actions:upgrade:dry-run` | Preview action upgrades without modifying files |
| `task actions:versions` | Show SHA-pinned GitHub Actions with their version tags |

### Changelog

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task changelog:check` | Validate changelog format and consistency |

### Clean

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task clean:all` | Remove all build artifacts and caches |
| `task clean:build` | Clean build artifacts (hatch only) |
| `task clean:todo` | Archive completed TODO items |

### Commit

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task commit:check` | Validate commit messages in a range (default HEAD) |

### Db

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task db:reset` | Reset the local development database |

### Deps

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task deps:outdated` | Check for outdated dependencies |
| `task deps:update-comments` | Update version comments in pyproject.toml to match installed versions |
| `task deps:upgrade` | Upgrade dependencies (usage: task deps:upgrade, or: task deps:upgrade -- ruff 0.9.0) |
| `task deps:versions` | Show installed and latest versions of all project dependencies |

### Docs

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task docs:build` | Build documentation (strict mode) |
| `task docs:commands` | Regenerate the command reference page (docs/reference/commands.md) |
| `task docs:commands:check` | Check if the command reference page is stale (exit 1 if outdated) |
| `task docs:serve` | Serve documentation with live-reload |

### Doctor

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task doctor:env` | Run environment health check |
| `task doctor:repo` | Run repository health checks |

### Env

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task env:prune` | Remove ALL Hatch environments |
| `task env:reset` | Remove and recreate the default Hatch environment |
| `task env:show` | Show all Hatch environments and scripts |

### Fmt

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task fmt:check` | Check formatting without applying |
| `task fmt:markdown` | Format Markdown files (table alignment via prettier) |

### Issues

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task issues:check` | Flag stale Resolved entries in docs/known-issues.md |

### Labels

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task labels:apply` | Apply GitHub labels (usage: task labels:apply -- --set baseline \[--repo OWNER/REPO\] \[--dry-run\]) |
| `task labels:baseline` | Apply baseline labels to the current repo |
| `task labels:dry-run` | Preview baseline labels without applying |
| `task labels:full` | Apply extended labels to the current repo |

### Lint

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task lint:fix` | Auto-fix linting issues |

### Pre-Commit

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task pre-commit:hook` | Run a single hook on all files (usage: task pre-commit:hook -- ruff) |
| `task pre-commit:install` | Install all pre-commit hooks (pre-commit, commit-msg, pre-push) |
| `task pre-commit:run` | Run pre-commit on all files |
| `task pre-commit:update` | Update pre-commit hooks to latest versions |

### Test

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task test:cov` | Run tests with coverage |
| `task test:integration` | Run integration tests only |
| `task test:k` | Run tests matching keyword (usage: task test:k -- my_test) |
| `task test:lf` | Rerun only last-failed tests |
| `task test:matrix` | Run tests across all Python versions (3.11â€“3.13) |
| `task test:matrix:cov` | Run tests with coverage across all Python versions |
| `task test:unit` | Run unit tests only |
| `task test:v` | Run tests with verbose output |
| `task test:x` | Stop on first test failure |

### Todo

| Command | Description |
| ------- | ----------- |
| `task todo:check` | Find and report TODO items across the codebase |

## Scripts

Standalone utilities in `scripts/`. Run with `python scripts/<name>.py` or via the corresponding Taskfile shortcut.

### `apply_labels.py`

**File:** [`scripts/apply_labels.py`](../../scripts/apply_labels.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: apply_labels.py [-h] --set {baseline,extended} [--repo REPO]
                           [--dry-run] [--version]

    Apply GitHub labels to a repository from a JSON label set.

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --set {baseline,extended}
      --repo REPO           OWNER/REPO; default is current repo
      --dry-run
      --version             show program's version number and exit
    ```

### `archive_todos.py`

**File:** [`scripts/archive_todos.py`](../../scripts/archive_todos.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: archive_todos [-h] [--version] [--dry-run] [--quiet] [--no-backup]
                         [--todo-file TODO_FILE] [--archive-file ARCHIVE_FILE]

    Archive completed TODO items from docs/notes/todo.md.

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
      --dry-run             Show what would change without modifying files
      --quiet, -q           Suppress informational output (errors still shown)
      --no-backup           Skip creating .bak files before writing
      --todo-file TODO_FILE
                            Path to todo file (default: C:\Users\ID136\OneDrive\Do
                            cuments\VSCode\Workspaces\simple-python-
                            boilerplate\docs\notes\todo.md)
      --archive-file ARCHIVE_FILE
                            Path to archive file (default: C:\Users\ID136\OneDrive
                            \Documents\VSCode\Workspaces\simple-python-
                            boilerplate\docs\notes\archive.md)
    ```

### `bootstrap.py`

**File:** [`scripts/bootstrap.py`](../../scripts/bootstrap.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: bootstrap [-h] [--version] [--skip-hooks] [--skip-test-matrix]
                     [--dry-run] [--quiet]

    One-command setup for fresh clones.

    options:
      -h, --help          show this help message and exit
      --version           show program's version number and exit
      --skip-hooks        Skip pre-commit hook installation
      --skip-test-matrix  Skip creating test.py3.x environments (faster setup)
      --dry-run           Show what would happen without making changes
      --quiet, -q         Suppress informational output (errors and warnings still
                          shown)
    ```

    ```text
    usage: changelog_check.py [-h] [--version] [--verbose | --quiet]

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
      --verbose, -v         Show versions that are in sync
      --quiet, -q           Suppress output; exit code only (for CI)
      --changelog-path CHANGELOG_PATH
    ```

### `check_known_issues.py`

**File:** [`scripts/check_known_issues.py`](../../scripts/check_known_issues.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: check_known_issues.py [-h] [--days N] [--issues-path PATH] [--json]
                                 [-q] [--version]

    Check docs/known-issues.md for stale resolved entries. Exits non-zero if any
    entry has been in the Resolved table longer than --days.

    options:
      -h, --help          show this help message and exit
      --days N            Max age in days for resolved entries (default: 90)
      --issues-path PATH  Path to known-issues.md (default: docs/known-issues.md)
      --json              Output results as JSON
      -q, --quiet         Suppress output; exit code only
      --version           show program's version number and exit
    ```

### `check_todos.py`

**File:** [`scripts/check_todos.py`](../../scripts/check_todos.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: check_todos.py [-h] [--version] [--pattern PATTERN] [--count | --json]
                          [--quiet] [--exclude EXCLUDE]

    Scan for TODO (template users) comments in the repository.

    options:
      -h, --help         show this help message and exit
      --version          show program's version number and exit
      --pattern PATTERN  Text pattern to search for (default: "TODO (template
                         users)")
      --count            Only print the count of TODOs found
      --json             Output results as JSON (for CI integration)
      --quiet, -q        Suppress output (exit code still indicates result)
      --exclude EXCLUDE  Additional path prefixes to exclude (relative to project
                         root)

    exit codes:
      0  No TODOs found (template fully customized)
      1  TODOs remain (useful as a CI gate)
    ```

### `clean.py`

**File:** [`scripts/clean.py`](../../scripts/clean.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: clean [-h] [--version] [--dry-run] [--quiet] [--include-venv] [--yes]

    Remove build artifacts and caches from the repository.

    options:
      -h, --help      show this help message and exit
      --version       show program's version number and exit
      --dry-run       Show what would be removed without deleting
      --quiet, -q     Suppress informational output (errors still shown)
      --include-venv  Also remove .venv* directories (requires confirmation)
      --yes, -y       Skip confirmation prompt for --include-venv

    exit codes:
      0  Clean completed successfully (or aborted by user)
      1  Error during cleanup
    ```

### `customize.py`

**File:** [`scripts/customize.py`](../../scripts/customize.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: customize.py [-h] [--version] [--dry-run] [--quiet] [--non-interactive]
                        [--project-name PROJECT_NAME]
                        [--package-name PACKAGE_NAME] [--author AUTHOR]
                        [--github-user GITHUB_USER] [--description DESCRIPTION]
                        [--cli-prefix CLI_PREFIX]
                        [--license {apache-2.0,mit,bsd-3-clause}]
                        [--strip [DIR ...]] [--force]
                        [--enable-workflows OWNER/REPO]

    Interactive project customization — replaces boilerplate placeholders with your project's values.

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
      --dry-run             Show what would change without modifying any files
      --quiet, -q           Suppress informational output (errors still shown)
      --non-interactive     Skip interactive prompts; use CLI arguments for all
                            values
      --project-name PROJECT_NAME
                            Project name (lowercase-hyphenated)
      --package-name PACKAGE_NAME
                            Python package name (underscored; auto-derived from
                            --project-name if omitted)
      --author AUTHOR       Author name
      --github-user GITHUB_USER
                            GitHub username or organization
      --description DESCRIPTION
                            One-line project description
      --cli-prefix CLI_PREFIX
                            CLI command prefix (default: initials of project name)
      --license {apache-2.0,mit,bsd-3-clause}
                            License to use (default: apache-2.0)
      --strip [DIR ...]     Optional directories to remove. Choices: db,
                            experiments, var, docs-templates, container
      --force               Skip the already-customized safety check
      --enable-workflows OWNER/REPO
                            Enable all GitHub workflows by replacing
                            'YOURNAME/YOURREPO' placeholder. Pass your repo slug
                            (e.g. 'myorg/myproject'). Runs only this operation.

    examples:
      python scripts/customize.py
      python scripts/customize.py --dry-run
      python scripts/customize.py --non-interactive \
          --project-name my-project --author "Jane Doe" \
          --github-user janedoe
    ```

### `dep_versions.py`

**File:** [`scripts/dep_versions.py`](../../scripts/dep_versions.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: dep_versions [-h] [--version] {show,update-comments,upgrade} ...

    Dependency version manager for pyproject.toml and requirements files.

    positional arguments:
      {show,update-comments,upgrade}
                            Available commands
        show                Show installed and latest versions of all dependencies
                            (default).
        update-comments     Update inline version comments in pyproject.toml and
                            requirements*.txt files. Adds descriptions and
                            versions to lines that have no comment.
        upgrade             Upgrade dependencies via pip.

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
    ```

### `doctor.py`

**File:** [`scripts/doctor.py`](../../scripts/doctor.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: doctor [-h] [--version] [--output OUTPUT] [--markdown | --json |
                  --quiet]

    Print a diagnostics bundle for bug reports.

    options:
      -h, --help           show this help message and exit
      --version            show program's version number and exit
      --output, -o OUTPUT  Write output to file (e.g., var/doctor.txt)
      --markdown           Output as markdown (for GitHub issues)
      --json               Output as JSON (machine-readable)
      --quiet, -q          Print a one-line summary only
    ```

### `env_doctor.py`

**File:** [`scripts/env_doctor.py`](../../scripts/env_doctor.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: env_doctor.py [-h] [--version] [--strict] [--no-color] [--json]

    Quick environment health check for development.

    options:
      -h, --help  show this help message and exit
      --version   show program's version number and exit
      --strict    Treat optional tool warnings as failures
      --no-color  Disable colored output
      --json      Output results as JSON (for CI integration)
    ```

### `generate_command_reference.py`

**File:** [`scripts/generate_command_reference.py`](../../scripts/generate_command_reference.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: generate_command_reference [-h] [--check] [--dry-run] [--version]

    Generate docs/reference/commands.md from project commands.

    options:
      -h, --help  show this help message and exit
      --check     Exit 1 if the generated file differs from the existing one (CI
                  mode).
      --dry-run   Print generated Markdown to stdout instead of writing to disk.
      --version   show program's version number and exit
    ```

### `repo_doctor.py`

**File:** [`scripts/repo_doctor.py`](../../scripts/repo_doctor.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: repo_doctor.py [-h] [--version] [--missing] [--staged] [--diff RANGE]
                          [--category CAT] [--min-level {info,warn}]
                          [--include-info] [--profile NAME] [--no-hints]
                          [--no-links] [--fix] [--no-color]

    Warn-only repo doctor checks (missing files, deletions, config presence).

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
      --missing             Report missing files/dirs in working tree.
      --staged              Report staged deletions (index).
      --diff RANGE          Report deletions in a git diff range, e.g.
                            "origin/main...HEAD".
      --category CAT        Only show rules matching this category (e.g. ci,
                            hooks, security).
      --min-level {info,warn}
                            Minimum severity to display (default: warn). Use
                            'info' to include everything.
      --include-info        Include info-level checks (shorthand for --min-level
                            info).
      --profile NAME        Load additional rules from repo_doctor.d/NAME.toml
                            (repeatable; 'all' loads everything).
      --no-hints            Hide hint lines.
      --no-links            Hide link/reference lines.
      --fix                 Show auto-fix commands for rules that define one.
      --no-color            Disable colored output.
    ```

### `workflow_versions.py`

**File:** [`scripts/workflow_versions.py`](../../scripts/workflow_versions.py)

??? note "Full `--help` output"

    ```text
    usage: workflow_versions [-h] [--version] [--color | --no-color] [--quiet]
                             {show,update-comments,upgrade} ...

    Show and update version comments on SHA-pinned GitHub Actions in
    .github/workflows/.

    positional arguments:
      {show,update-comments,upgrade}
                            Available commands
        show                Show all SHA-pinned actions with comment and resolved
                            tags (default).
        update-comments     Update inline version comments and add action
                            descriptions. Comments are rewritten as '#
                            <description> (vX.Y.Z)'. Requires GitHub API access.
        upgrade             Upgrade actions to their latest release (or a specific
                            version).

    options:
      -h, --help            show this help message and exit
      --version             show program's version number and exit
      --color               Force colored output.
      --no-color            Disable colored output.
      --quiet, -q           Suppress table output. Exit non-zero if issues are
                            found (CI mode).
    ```

    ```text
    usage: check_nul_bytes.py [-h] [--version] [files ...]

    Fail if any file contains NUL (0x00) bytes.

    positional arguments:
      files       Files to check (passed by pre-commit).

    options:
      -h, --help  show this help message and exit
      --version   show program's version number and exit
    ```

## Shared Modules

Internal modules in `scripts/` used by multiple scripts. Not intended to be run directly.

| Module | Description |
| ------ | ----------- |
| [`_progress.py`](../../scripts/_progress.py) | Progress bar and spinner utilities (Unicode/ASCII) |

## Hatch Environments

Managed by [Hatch](https://hatch.pypa.io/). Enter with `hatch shell` or run commands with `hatch run <cmd>`.

```text
Standalone
+----------------------------------------------+
| Name    | Type    | Features | Scripts       |
|---------+---------+----------+---------------|
| default | virtual | dev      | archive-todos |
|         |         |          | check         |
|         |         |          | fmt           |
|         |         |          | fmt-check     |
|         |         |          | lint          |
|         |         |          | lint-fix      |
|         |         |          | test          |
|         |         |          | test-cov      |
|         |         |          | typecheck     |
|---------+---------+----------+---------------|
| docs    | virtual | docs     | archive-todos |
|         |         |          | build         |
|         |         |          | check         |
|         |         |          | fmt           |
|         |         |          | fmt-check     |
|         |         |          | lint          |
|         |         |          | lint-fix      |
|         |         |          | serve         |
|         |         |          | test          |
|         |         |          | test-cov      |
|         |         |          | typecheck     |
+----------------------------------------------+
Matrices
+---------------------------------------------------------+
| Name | Type    | Envs        | Features | Scripts       |
|------+---------+-------------+----------+---------------|
| test | virtual | test.py3.11 | test     | archive-todos |
|      |         | test.py3.12 |          | check         |
|      |         | test.py3.13 |          | cov           |
|      |         |             |          | fmt           |
|      |         |             |          | fmt-check     |
|      |         |             |          | lint          |
|      |         |             |          | lint-fix      |
|      |         |             |          | run           |
|      |         |             |          | test          |
|      |         |             |          | test-cov      |
|      |         |             |          | typecheck     |
+---------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Quick Reference

Common operations and the fastest way to run them.

| What | Command |
| ---- | ------- |
| Run all quality checks | `task check` |
| Run tests | `task test` |
| Run tests with coverage | `task test:cov` |
| Lint + auto-fix | `task lint:fix` |
| Format code | `task fmt` |
| Type check | `task typecheck` |
| Serve docs locally | `task docs:serve` |
| Show dependency versions | `task deps:versions` |
| Update dep comments | `task deps:update-comments` |
| Show action versions | `task actions:versions` |
| Interactive commit | `task commit` |
| Enter dev shell | `task shell` |
| Bootstrap fresh clone | `task bootstrap` |
| Clean build artifacts | `task clean:all` |
| Regenerate command reference | `task docs:commands` |
| Check reference freshness | `task docs:commands:check` |
